_note: Director Score frame markers captured by screenMaster.initScreenList at boot. The Score is NOT a runtime timeline: the playhead is pinned on frame 1 (loop frame behavior); each marker's static sprite layout is snapshotted into an objScreen and composited on demand via movieMaster.goScreen(sym, #fade). Port these as Scene/state classes.
stageSize_note: play area = the Director stage rect (gStageSize); rooms are 18x9 tiles @32px = 576x288.

screens.label: titleScreen
screens.wiring: boot lands here (movieMaster.start -> goScreen(#titleScreen)). Buttons: intro->goSequence(#intro), gameScreen, reloadGame->goScreen(#gameScreen,#loadGame), instructions, credits, copyright/links->URL

screens.label: animScreenSingleCutScene
screens.wiring: plays a single selected cut scene (file moved into cut_scene_to_play/)

screens.label: gameScreen
screens.wiring: THE gameplay screen. Enters via cutSceneMaster.finish then game loop; <-> ingameMenu

screens.label: initMap
screens.wiring: map initialization marker (not a user-facing screen)

screens.label: 3DMode
screens.wiring: g3DMode=false everywhere; dead branch, do not port

screen_layout_extraction.reason: VWSC Score uses a pooled/pointer-encoded sprite format (data pool [15120,96192], 3773-entry frame offset table, per-frame pointer-reference records). Field-accurate decode is high-effort, low-value vs reconstructing screens from menu data + extracted background bitmaps.
screen_layout_extraction.fallback: Reconstruct each screen from objMenu text-definitions + extracted/engine/bitmaps backgrounds; refine positions visually during port.
